title: Offline-first Architecture
objectives: Design an app that works without network
Read/write through a local source of truth
Sync in the background with conflict rules

sections.markdown: Offline-first treats the **local database as the source of truth**. The UI reads and writes locally; a background sync layer talks to the server. Users get instant feedback and the app keeps working with no connection.

sections.code:
UI --(observe)--> Local DB (source of truth)
         |
         v
    Sync engine <---> Network
    - read-through:   fetch → cache → emit
    - write-through:  write local → enqueue outbox → push

sections.markdown: Offline-first isn't 'add caching' — it's a different mental model. The UI never blocks on the network. That flips your architecture: repositories emit Streams from the local store, and network is a background concern that eventually syncs.
Silently dropping user writes when offline. Enqueue them in a durable outbox and surface pending/failed state; never lie to the user about a save that didn't reach the server.

flashcards.front: What is the source of truth in offline-first?
flashcards.back: The local database. The UI reads/writes locally; sync is a background concern.
Persist the change locally AND queue it for the server, retrying on network.
Choose a policy: last-write-wins, per-field merge, or CRDT — and surface it to the user when needed.

quiz.q: In offline-first, the UI reads from…
quiz.explain: Local store is authoritative; the UI never blocks on network.

quiz.q: A safe way to handle offline writes is…
options: Persist + enqueue in an outbox, retry
quiz.explain: Durable queue + retries + honest UI status.
Pick a policy consistent with your data.

challenges.text: Add read-through caching to one repository.
Implement write-through with a durable outbox that retries on connectivity.
Add optimistic UI + rollback on server rejection.
Design a merge/CRDT strategy for two devices editing the same record.

interviewQuestions: What does offline-first mean at the architecture level?
Why is the local DB the source of truth?
How do you handle offline writes safely?
How do you surface sync/failure state to users?
